mission: Freeze the authoritative sandbox-account, test-account, environment-credential, vault-ingest, and owner-chain model for Vecells external integrations across mock-now execution and later real-provider onboarding.

non_negotiable_rules.summary: No real credential or secret may be committed to the repository, markdown examples, screenshots, or Playwright artifacts.
Browsers may never hold long-lived provider secrets; browser automation consumes brokered values only during gated dry runs.
Environments do not inherit secrets from each other; every widen, redirect, key, sender, mailbox, or number change requires explicit governance.
Shared production credentials must always have a named owner, backup owner, approver, rotation proof, and revocation path.
Test users, sandpit accounts, and shared-dev fixtures may never silently gain production privilege or live patient authority.

assumptions.summary: Shared dev, sandpit, and integration environments use one managed non-production vault boundary with environment-specific paths and access policy.
All real later credentials land first in a temporary quarantine before ingest into the permanent vault or metadata registry.
Live dry-run browser automation defaults to redacted screenshots, no traces, and no video until seq_024-025 deliberately widen capture posture.

role_catalog.ROLE_IDENTITY_PARTNER_MANAGER: Owns NHS login and identity-partner account posture.
role_catalog.ROLE_INTEROPERABILITY_LEAD: Owns NHS and partner interoperability onboarding across IM1, MESH, PDS, and route proofs.
role_catalog.ROLE_COMMUNICATIONS_PLATFORM_LEAD: Owns telephony, notifications, transcript, and scanning provider posture.
role_catalog.ROLE_BOOKING_DOMAIN_LEAD: Owns booking-supplier policy and confirmation-truth gates.
role_catalog.ROLE_NETWORK_COORDINATION_OWNER: Owns network-capacity and practice acknowledgement bindings.
role_catalog.ROLE_PHARMACY_PARTNER_OWNER: Owns pharmacy directory and transport partner posture.
role_catalog.ROLE_PHARMACY_DOMAIN_LEAD: Owns pharmacy outcome and professional-route semantics.
role_catalog.ROLE_EMBEDDED_CHANNEL_LEAD: Owns deferred NHS App channel onboarding posture.
role_catalog.ROLE_AI_GOVERNANCE_LEAD: Owns future-optional model vendor onboarding posture.
role_catalog.ROLE_PARTNER_ONBOARDING_LEAD: Creates partner account requests and coordinates capture sessions.
role_catalog.ROLE_SECURITY_LEAD: Owns secret-handling policy, vault law, and revocation approval.
role_catalog.ROLE_OPERATIONS_LEAD: Owns live operational runbooks, phone-number control, and mailbox escalation posture.
role_catalog.ROLE_GOVERNANCE_LEAD: Approves regulated or policy-sensitive widening actions.
role_catalog.ROLE_MANUFACTURER_CSO: Approves clinically sensitive transcript and safety-bearing provider changes.
role_catalog.ROLE_DPO: Approves privacy-sensitive enrichment or model-vendor access changes.
role_catalog.ROLE_PROGRAMME_ARCHITECT: Owns exact publication, route, and environment-parity law.
role_catalog.ROLE_SUPPORT_LEAD: Owns wrong-recipient, bounced-delivery, and recovery pathways.

storage_backends.summary: Developer-machine secret store outside the repository and outside captured test artifacts.
Short-lived CI secret injection, reset every run, never written into build output.
Synthetic test-user, fixture-pack, sender, and reserved-number registry for shared non-production rehearsal.
Managed non-production vault for simulator credentials, shared webhook secrets, and mailbox placeholders.
Write-only temporary landing zone for newly captured live credentials before ingest into the permanent vault.
Registry for redirect inventories, sender identities, phone numbers, client-registration metadata, and site-link placeholders.
Non-production HSM or KMS-backed custody for private keys and certificate material.
Pre-production vault for live-like secret handling before release widening.
Production vault with dual-control rotation, revocation, and access-audit obligations.
Production HSM or KMS-backed custody for identity, mailbox, and signing private keys.

GATE_EXTERNAL_TO_FOUNDATION.label: External readiness to foundation gate
GATE_EXTERNAL_TO_FOUNDATION.summary: No live-provider capture may claim Phase 0 readiness until the external-readiness chain clears.

LIVE_GATE_NHS_LOGIN_PARTNER_APPROVED.label: NHS login partner approval
LIVE_GATE_NHS_LOGIN_PARTNER_APPROVED.summary: Real NHS login credentials require approved partner onboarding and named approver identity.

LIVE_GATE_REDIRECT_URI_REVIEW.label: Redirect and environment review
LIVE_GATE_REDIRECT_URI_REVIEW.summary: Redirect URI, route family, and environment additions require review against the canonical route inventory.

LIVE_GATE_IDENTITY_SESSION_PARITY.label: Identity and session parity proof
LIVE_GATE_IDENTITY_SESSION_PARITY.summary: No real identity credential is admissible until local-session proof and callback parity are current.

LIVE_GATE_TELEPHONY_RECORDING_APPROVED.label: Telephony recording and escalation review
LIVE_GATE_TELEPHONY_RECORDING_APPROVED.summary: Live telephony numbers and recording credentials require safety, retention, and urgent-escalation sign-off.

LIVE_GATE_TRANSCRIPT_SAFETY_REVIEW.label: Transcript and derived-facts safety review
LIVE_GATE_TRANSCRIPT_SAFETY_REVIEW.summary: Transcript or scan credentials stay blocked until clinical-safety and masking policy review is current.

LIVE_GATE_VENDOR_SENDER_REVIEW.label: Sender identity and wrong-recipient review
LIVE_GATE_VENDOR_SENDER_REVIEW.summary: Email or SMS sender identities require wrong-recipient, dispute, and support runbook sign-off.

LIVE_GATE_IM1_SCAL_APPROVED.label: IM1 and SCAL approval
LIVE_GATE_IM1_SCAL_APPROVED.summary: GP supplier and pairing credentials remain placeholders until IM1 and SCAL evidence is current.

LIVE_GATE_SUPPLIER_CAPABILITY_REVIEW.label: Supplier capability evidence review
LIVE_GATE_SUPPLIER_CAPABILITY_REVIEW.summary: Live booking or GP supplier accounts require explicit capability-matrix and fallback review.

LIVE_GATE_NETWORK_FEED_TRUST_REVIEW.label: Network feed trust review
LIVE_GATE_NETWORK_FEED_TRUST_REVIEW.summary: Capacity feeds require source freshness, trust posture, and policy tuple review.

LIVE_GATE_CROSS_ORG_TRANSPORT_APPROVED.label: Cross-organisation transport approval
LIVE_GATE_CROSS_ORG_TRANSPORT_APPROVED.summary: Mailbox or transport credentials need cross-organisation approval and minimum-necessary payload review.

LIVE_GATE_PHARMACY_DIRECTORY_REVIEW.label: Pharmacy directory access-path review
LIVE_GATE_PHARMACY_DIRECTORY_REVIEW.summary: Directory accounts remain blocked until access-path and patient-choice compliance review are current.

LIVE_GATE_PHARMACY_TRANSPORT_REVIEW.label: Pharmacy transport and urgent-return review
LIVE_GATE_PHARMACY_TRANSPORT_REVIEW.summary: Transport credentials require professional-route ownership and redispatch policy sign-off.

LIVE_GATE_PHARMACY_OUTCOME_REVIEW.label: Pharmacy outcome acceptance review
LIVE_GATE_PHARMACY_OUTCOME_REVIEW.summary: Outcome credentials require parser quality, reconciliation, and manual-review thresholds.

LIVE_GATE_PDS_LEGAL_BASIS_APPROVED.label: PDS legal basis and feature-flag approval
LIVE_GATE_PDS_LEGAL_BASIS_APPROVED.summary: PDS access is blocked until legal basis, tenant feature flag, and enrichment posture are approved.

LIVE_GATE_NHS_APP_DEFERRED_ONLY.label: NHS App deferred-channel gate
LIVE_GATE_NHS_APP_DEFERRED_ONLY.summary: Embedded-channel placeholders stay non-live until the deferred Phase 7 channel opens.

LIVE_GATE_ASSISTIVE_INTENDED_USE_REVIEW.label: Assistive intended-use and subprocessor review
LIVE_GATE_ASSISTIVE_INTENDED_USE_REVIEW.summary: Future model-vendor secrets stay blocked until intended use, rollback, and subprocessor review are complete.

identity_auth.title: NHS login identity rail
source_refs: blueprint/phase-2-the-identity-and-echoes.md#2B. NHS login bridge and local session engine
identity_auth.notes: Auth success never implies writable authority; redirect and key changes are route-law changes, not mere config edits.

patient_data_enrichment.title: Optional PDS enrichment seam
source_refs: blueprint/phase-2-the-identity-and-echoes.md#2C. Patient linkage, demographic confidence, and optional PDS enrichment
patient_data_enrichment.notes: This family stays optional and gated by legal basis, feature flags, and minimal-data disclosure.

telephony.title: Telephony, IVR, and recording rail
source_refs: blueprint/phase-2-the-identity-and-echoes.md#2E. Telephony edge, IVR choreography, and call-session persistence
blueprint/phase-2-the-identity-and-echoes.md#2F. Caller verification, voice capture, transcript stub, and SMS continuation
telephony.notes: Live phone-number ownership and recording policy must stay explicit and revocable.

transcription.title: Transcript processing provider
source_refs: blueprint/phase-2-the-identity-and-echoes.md#2F. Caller verification, voice capture, transcript stub, and SMS continuation
transcription.notes: Transcript credentials stay blocked until derived-facts and masking posture are reviewed.

sms.title: SMS continuation delivery
sms.notes: SMS remains optional and must stay segregated from email, telephony, and production patient-contact authority.

email.title: Email and secure-link notification rail
email.notes: Email sender and webhook posture must stay compatible with wrong-recipient repair and secure-link truth.

malware_scanning.title: Artifact scanning provider
malware_scanning.notes: Scanner credentials must stay quarantined from ordinary file-upload traces and seed packs.

gp_system.title: GP system and IM1 programme boundary
gp_system.notes: IM1 credentials and supplier-path credentials remain distinct and may not silently inherit each other.

booking_supplier.title: Booking supplier boundary
source_refs: blueprint/phase-4-the-booking-engine.md#4B. Provider capability matrix and adapter seam
booking_supplier.notes: Booking supplier credentials remain adapter-bound and never migrate into browser-delivered surfaces.

network_capacity.title: Network capacity partner feed
source_refs: blueprint/phase-5-the-network-horizon.md#5C. Enhanced Access policy engine and network capacity ingestion
network_capacity.notes: Feed credentials are non-authoritative without current freshness, trust, and fallback review.

messaging_transport.title: Cross-organisation messaging transport
messaging_transport.notes: Mailbox, certificate, and endpoint changes are transport-truth changes and require revocation proof.

pharmacy_directory.title: Pharmacy directory seam
source_refs: blueprint/phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6C. Pharmacy discovery, provider choice, and directory abstraction
pharmacy_directory.notes: Directory accounts remain separate from dispatch or outcome credentials so patient-choice review stays explicit.

pharmacy_transport.title: Pharmacy dispatch transport
source_refs: blueprint/phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6D. Referral pack composer, dispatch adapters, and transport contract
pharmacy_transport.notes: Dispatch transport credentials must stay separate from authoritative dispatch proof and urgent-return policy.

pharmacy_outcome.title: Pharmacy outcome observation
source_refs: blueprint/phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6F. Outcome ingest, Update Record observation, and reconciliation
pharmacy_outcome.notes: Outcome credentials stay separate from parser or reconciliation truth so weak matches remain reviewable.

embedded_channel.title: Deferred NHS App embedded channel
source_refs: blueprint/phase-7-inside-the-nhs-app.md#7H. Sandpit, AOS, SCAL, and operational delivery pipeline
embedded_channel.notes: This family stays deferred and placeholder-only until the Phase 7 gate opens.

model_vendor.title: Future-optional assistive vendor boundary
model_vendor.notes: Future-optional only; no live vendor secret is admissible until intended-use, rollback, and subprocessor posture are approved.

family_summary.dependency_title: GP system and IM1 programme boundary

family_summary.environments: local_mock, ci_mock, shared_dev, sandpit, integration, preprod, production

change_control_rules.evidence: Updated redirect matrix, route-family owner confirmation, dry-run callback proof, approval tuple

change_control_rules.summary: Client secret, private key, certificate, or mailbox credential rotation
change_control_rules.evidence: Rotation settlement record, vault version diff, replay and callback verification, revocation proof

change_control_rules.summary: Sandpit to integration, preprod, or production environment addition
change_control_rules.evidence: Environment parity checklist, control-plane publication proof, gate clearance, fresh runbook binding
Wrong-recipient or wrong-number drill, support path confirmation, rollback number or sender plan
Transport authenticity proof, endpoint registry diff, replay-safe redrive plan
Revocation bundle, degraded-mode posture confirmation, published fallback activation, audit export

runbook_steps: Confirm the target family, environment, named approver, and live gates before touching any provider portal or partner email.
Capture credentials or metadata only into the temporary quarantine or metadata review queue; never paste directly into the repo, chat, ticket text, or markdown.
Redact screenshots immediately and keep Playwright traces, videos, and console logs disabled unless the value path is demonstrably secret-free.
Ingest secret material into the correct vault or HSM path, then bind only the reference handle into runtime manifests or partner metadata registries.
Run post-ingest verification: ownership chain, gate references, redirect or endpoint parity, workload access policy, and audit-event emission.
Record rotation or revocation evidence in the external access lifecycle ledger and publish the updated runtime or metadata tuple only after approval.
On incident, revoke in the provider console first when necessary, rotate vault material second, invalidate runtime handles third, then append the incident evidence pack.

secret_classification_matrix.notes: Partner console identities are governed metadata and may never be the only proof of runtime authority.

secret_classification_matrix.notes: Service principals are always runtime-only and injected through workload-bound secret retrieval.

secret_classification_matrix.notes: Phone numbers are operational identifiers and must never silently cross mock, test, and live cohorts.

global_rules: No real provider secrets enter the seed plan.
Local and CI seed material resets independently; shared-dev resets are explicit and audited.
Playwright and CI consume brokered mock references, never repo fixtures with live-like secret values.

seed_plans.notes: Booking supplier credentials remain adapter-bound and never migrate into browser-delivered surfaces.
Email sender and webhook posture must stay compatible with wrong-recipient repair and secure-link truth.
Auth success never implies writable authority; redirect and key changes are route-law changes, not mere config edits.
Scanner credentials must stay quarantined from ordinary file-upload traces and seed packs.
Mailbox, certificate, and endpoint changes are transport-truth changes and require revocation proof.
Directory accounts remain separate from dispatch or outcome credentials so patient-choice review stays explicit.
Outcome credentials stay separate from parser or reconciliation truth so weak matches remain reviewable.
Dispatch transport credentials must stay separate from authoritative dispatch proof and urgent-return policy.
SMS remains optional and must stay segregated from email, telephony, and production patient-contact authority.
Live phone-number ownership and recording policy must stay explicit and revocable.
Transcript credentials stay blocked until derived-facts and masking posture are reviewed.
